title: FedEx Places First Order for 15 Workhorse W56 Electric Step Vans
text:
FedEx Orders 15 Workhorse W56 Electric Step Vans

Date: September 9, 2024

Key Details

FedEx has placed its first purchase order for 15 Workhorse W56 step vans, scheduled for 2024 delivery. This milestone follows successful testing demonstrating the vehicle meets FedEx operational requirements.

Performance Metrics

During real-world testing, the W56 achieved 31 MPGe—significantly more efficient than the national average of 7 MPG for traditional delivery trucks. Based on annual mileage of approximately 31,875 miles per vehicle, the fleet is expected to avoid roughly 607 metric tons of tailpipe emissions yearly.

Environmental Impact

The addition supports FedEx's commitment to achieve carbon neutral global operations by 2040, with plans to transition their entire parcel delivery fleet to zero-emission vehicles by that target date.

Company Perspective

Workhorse CEO Rick Dauch emphasized the significance: This milestone demonstrates our highly efficient and high-quality EV chassis and body along with our excellent engineering and operational capabilities. The company highlighted its competitive advantage in building complete vehicles rather than components alone.

About the W56

The vehicle features an efficient eAxle electric drivetrain with regenerative braking, designed specifically for stop-and-go delivery routes typical in last-mile operations.
